title: Pan-Seared Dabo with Spinach and Crispy Ethiopian Spices
description: A unique fusion of Japanese flavors with Ethiopian spices, featuring crispy dabo and fresh spinach.
instructions: 1. In a shallow dish, whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, and honey to make the marinade.
2. Add the dabo to the marinade and let it sit for 15 minutes.
3. Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
4. Remove the dabo from the marinade and sear both sides until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes per side.
5. Transfer the dabo to a plate and set aside.
6. In the same skillet, add the crispy Ethiopian spices and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
7. Add the chopped spinach to the skillet and cook until wilted, about 2-3 minutes.
8. Return the dabo to the skillet and stir to combine with the spinach and spices.
9. Drizzle the sesame oil over the top and serve immediately.
10. Garnish with additional crispy Ethiopian spices, if desired.
